title: The Love-Sick Boy.
content:
When first my old, old love I knew,
    My bosom welled with joy;
    My riches at her feet I threw;
    I was a love-sick boy!
    No terms seemed too extravagant
    Upon her to employ 
    I used to mope, and sigh, and pant,
    Just like a love-sick boy!

    But joy incessant palls the sense;
    And love, unchanged will cloy,
    And she became a bore intense
    Unto her love-sick boy!
    With fitful glimmer burnt my flame,
    And I grew cold and coy,
    At last, one morning, I became
    Another's love-sick boy!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Schwenck Gilbert delivers a powerful performance in "The Love-Sick Boy."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
